summary: Matched additional iftime timer/countdown code by making small codegen-shaping edits in src/melee/if/iftime.c and by splitting a broad ifTime_803F94E8 data symbol into individual string labels in GALE01 symbols. The changes are narrowly focused on decomp matching: introducing a local pointer alias to ifTime_data, preserving a call boundary through a dontinline-style wrapper, forcing a byte temporary for HSD_GObj_804D7849, adding PAD_STACK(8), and adjusting local variable ordering/types to reproduce target assembly.
what_changed: In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, replaced the single global data object ifTime_803F94E8 at .data:0x803F94E8 size 0xAD with six separate global string labels: lbl_803F94E8, lbl_803F9500, lbl_803F9530, lbl_803F953C, lbl_803F9548, and lbl_803F9570.

In ifTime_SetTime, introduced a local struct ifTime_data* x = &ifTime_data and used x->digits for selected accesses while leaving other ifTime_data.digits accesses intact, indicating a targeted register/addressing match rather than a broad refactor.
Added static inline int ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds_dontinline(void), which simply returns ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds().
In ifTime_UpdateCountdown/related timer logic, x->countdown_seconds is now assigned through ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds_dontinline() rather than calling ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds() directly.

In ifTime_UpdateTimers, reordered/adjusted locals, added u8 tmp and PAD_STACK(8), assigned tmp = HSD_GObj_804D7849, and passed tmp to HSD_GObjObject_80390A70 instead of passing HSD_GObj_804D7849 directly.
smart_moves: Used a narrow local pointer alias to ifTime_data only where needed, which is a common matching tactic for influencing register allocation without rewriting the function wholesale.

Preserved an otherwise trivial call boundary with a dontinline-named wrapper around ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds, suggesting the target binary required a separate call pattern or inhibited inlining at that site.
Introduced a u8 temporary for HSD_GObj_804D7849 before passing it to HSD_GObjObject_80390A70, likely to force the same load/extension/register behavior as the original code.
Added PAD_STACK(8) to align or reserve stack space for the target function frame while keeping the semantic code unchanged.
Split a large anonymous/global data blob into precise string symbols, improving the symbol map and making future string/data references more searchable and accurate.
decomp_lessons: For matching, seemingly redundant aliases like struct ifTime_data* x = &ifTime_data can be useful when only a subset of accesses need different codegen.
A trivial wrapper function can be an intentional matching device when the compiler would otherwise inline or emit a different direct call sequence.
Temporary variables with exact small integer types, such as u8 tmp, can be necessary to match loads, stores, and argument promotion behavior.
PAD_STACK is an accepted tactic for matching stack frame size/layout when semantics are already correct but the generated frame differs.

When a data symbol actually contains multiple strings, splitting it into per-string labels is preferable to keeping one oversized object, because it improves data typing and future cross-reference clarity.
naming_conventions: Existing project style keeps interface timer functions in Pascal-like module prefix form such as ifTime_SetTime, ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds, ifTime_UpdateCountdown, and ifTime_UpdateTimers.
The temporary wrapper was named ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds_dontinline, explicitly documenting that its purpose is codegen/inlining control rather than new game logic.
Unidentified data strings were named with lbl_803Fxxxx address-based labels rather than speculative semantic names.
Constants/macros retain uppercase names such as IFTIME_HOUR_SEP, IFTIME_HOUR_ONE, IFTIME_HOUR_TEN, IFTIME_ONES_DIGIT, and IFTIME_TENS_DIGIT.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Local pointer aliasing of a global struct to influence register allocation/addressing: struct ifTime_data* x = &ifTime_data.
Selective replacement of ifTime_data.digits[...] with x->digits[...] to tune only specific instructions.
Use of a static inline wrapper named dontinline to preserve a desired call shape for ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds.
Use of PAD_STACK(8) to force stack frame/layout agreement.
Use of a u8 temporary for HSD_GObj_804D7849 before passing it as an argument.
Local variable declaration ordering and introduction of tmp appear to be used as codegen levers.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
One issue comment was counted, but no body text was included in the context, so no reviewer intent or feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that use PAD_STACK for if/ interface functions.
Find other dontinline wrapper functions used solely for matching.
Search for local pointer aliases to global data structs used to tune codegen.
Inspect future iftime work for whether lbl_803F94E8 through lbl_803F9570 received semantic names.
Compare other symbol-map PRs that split large data objects into individual string labels.
